title: Verify historical origins claim
abstract: The note states jazz piano emerged in New Orleans in the early 20th century. This is broadly accurate but could be contested — jazz originated in New Orleans but the piano's specific role across multiple cities (e.g., Harlem stride) is worth noting.

title: Confirm named pioneers are representative
abstract: Jelly Roll Morton, Thelonious Monk, and Bill Evans are cited as pioneers. These are well-established figures; however, players like Art Tatum and McCoy Tyner are also highly significant and their omission is a matter of editorial scope, not factual error.

title: Note covers key substyles adequately
abstract: Stride, bebop, and modal jazz are listed as substyles. This selection is accurate and representative of major jazz piano traditions, though post-bop and free jazz are absent.

title: Dual role description is accurate
abstract: The description of jazz piano as both melodic voice and harmonic anchor is factually correct and clearly explained. No revision needed.

title: Content is factual and well-structured
abstract: The paragraph is concise, factually sound, and coherent. It meets the stated requirements of 3-5 sentences on a factual topic.
